data.title: Black Iron announces upsizing of LIFE offering
data.description:
• Black Iron (BKI:CA) on Wednesday said it has upsized the listed issuer financing exemption offering announced on April 6 by $200,000, from gross proceeds of up to $1.7M to gross proceeds of up to $1.9M. 
  • The LIFE offering continues to be conducted on a non-brokered basis at a price of $0.10 per unit of the company, for the issuance of up to an additional 2.6M units pursuant to the upsizing, bringing the maximum number of units issuable under the LIFE offering to 26.26M units if fully subscribed.
  • Each unit will consist of one immediately free trading common share in the capital of the company and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one common share of the company at a price of C$0.20 per common share for a period of 36 months following the closing date, commencing on the date that is 60 days from the closing date.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the units will be used for ongoing project and administrative expenditures, including permit renewal relating to the company’s Shymanivske Project and general corporate purposes and working capital as set out in the offering document.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Scienture granted 180-day extension by Nasdaq to regain compliance
data.description:
• Scienture (SCNX) on Wednesday said it has received notification from Nasdaq that it has approved the company's request for an additional 180-calendar day extension to regain compliance with the minimum closing bid price requirement of $1.00 per share.
  • The company now has until October 12, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • This current notification from Nasdaq has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of the company's common stock, which will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “SCNX.” 
  • SCNX +11.3% premarket to $0.3752.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Graham announces $50M investment from accounts advised by T. Rowe Price
data.description:
• Graham (GHM) announced on Wednesday the company has agreed to sell $50 million of shares (5%) of common stock to certain accounts advised by T. Rowe Price Investment Management, a global investment management organization.
  • T. Rowe Price accounts will acquire 599,808 shares of Graham common stock at $83.36 per share, based upon the 20-day average closing price of the company’s common stock on the New York Stock Exchange on April 13, 2026.
  • The transaction is expected to close on April 16, 2026.
  • Graham intends to use proceeds from the stock sale to further strengthen the company’s balance sheet and financial flexibility through debt repayment and help fund future investment in organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
  • GHM -1.06% premarket to $91.65. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple may use OLED screens for iPad Air next year: report
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is likely to switch up the display of the next version of the iPad Air and use OLED screens, Korea's ET News reported.

Shares of the iPhone maker were little changed in premarket trading on Wednesday.

Known as Organic Light-Emitting Diode, the OLED screens for the Apple tablet may come from Samsung Display (SSNLF), the news outlet added, citing industry sources. Current Apple products that have OLED displays include all of its iPhone line, all Apple Watch models, and various iPad models.

Mass production of the panels is set to occur near the end of the year or in January 2027, with the next iPad Air likely to be unveiled in early 2027.

Apple and Samsung did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Apple unveiled a new iPad Air, powered by its M4 processor, last month. It is available in 11-inch and 13-inch versions and comes in blue, purple, starlight, and space gray, with 128GB, 256GB, 512GB, and 1TB configurations. The 11-inch model starts at $599, while the 13-inch model starts at $799.

The new iPad Air, which runs on iPadOS 26 and has support for Apple Intelligence, became available for sale on March 11.

data.title: Credit spreads, regional banks flash 'damage contained' - SocGen
data.description:
Credit spreads (JNK) (HYG) (LQD) and U.S. regional banks (KRE) have moved into “damage‑contained” mode, signaling a pivot from second‑order fears and back to fundamentals, Societe Generale cross-asset strategist Manish Kabra wrote Wednesday.

"This credit stabilization pushed SG’s Cross‑Asset Momentum signal back to positive on Monday, after eight-consecutive bearish weeks," Kabra noted.

data.title: Morgan Stanley in charts: Wealth Management, Institutional Securities revenue rise in double digits Y/Y
Higher diesel prices don't hold back a partial trucking recovery in March
data.description:
The Cass Trucking and Freight Report is a monthly market update from Cass that tracks North American shipment volumes, freight spending, and truckload linehaul rates to gauge freight demand and pricing trends.

The March report showed freight volume still weak year over year but improving sequentially. The shipments index fell 4.5% from a year earlier but was up 3.0% month over month, extending February’s rebound and lifting the seasonally adjusted measure 1.0% month over month. Cass noted that the pace keeps alive the chance of a second-half recovery, even though the normal seasonal pattern would point to a roughly 5% year-over-year decline in April.

Freight spending also firmed up as the expenditures index increased 1.2% year over year in March, up from 2.1% in February, and rose 2.4% month over month on a seasonally adjusted basis. Cass attributed the gain mainly to the better shipment trend, not a sharp jump in pricing.

In terms of rates, the Truckload Linehaul Index slipped 0.5% month over month in March after a small February gain but still rose 1.8% year over year and 3.4% versus two years earlier. Cass pointed to some downward pressure on linehaul rates as capacity recovered from winter weather, offset by tighter capacity tied to higher diesel prices. The Iran-Israel-U.S. conflict began on February 28, while the biggest spike in diesel prices occurred later in March.

The Cass report is significant for many transportation stocks, including Knight-Swift (KNX), Werner Enterprises (WERN), J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), Schneider National (SNDR), Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL), Saia (SAIA), XPO (XPO), C.H. Robinson (CHRW), RXO (RXO), ArcBest (ARCB), Marten Transport (MRTN), Heartland Express (HTLD), Universal Logistics Holdings (ULH), TFI International (TFII), Hub Group (HUBG), Landstar (LSTR), and even large parcel carriers UPS (UPS) and FedEx (FDX).

data.title: Viant Technology to acquire TVision Insight for $40M
data.description:
• Viant Technology (DSP) announced on Wednesday it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire TVision Insight for $40M. 
  • The company said that the consideration consists of $22.5M in cash and $17.5M of shares of Viant's class A common stock. 
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2026. 
  • Rockefeller Capital Management served as exclusive investment banking advisor to TVision in connection with the transaction.
  • With this acquisition, Viant strengthens its AI-powered programmatic platform by integrating TVision's proprietary attention signals directly into its buying platform. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Morgan Stanley beats Q1 estimates; Institutional Securities hits $10.7B record revenue
Asml narrows 2026 sales outlook to €36B-€40B while targeting at least 80 low na euv units in 2027
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) Q1 2026

Management view

  • “For the quarter, total net sales came in at EUR 8.8 billion. That was within guidance.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board R.J.M. Dassen)

  • “Included in the EUR 8.8 billion was EUR 2.5 billion for Installed Base revenue. That was a little bit above the guidance.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “If you look at the gross margin for Q1, 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “Net income for the quarter, EUR 2.8 billion.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “We see that the semiconductor industry growth continued to solidify. This is still very much driven by investment in AI infrastructure.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Christophe Fouquet)

  • “We expect, in fact, that the supply will not meet the demand for the foreseeable future.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

  • “What our customers tell us is that they are sold out for 2026 and their supply constraint will last beyond 2026.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

  • “We believe we can drive an output for this year of at least 60 systems for EUV Low NA.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

Outlook

  • “For Q2, we expect EUR 8.4 billion to EUR 9 billion of total net sales.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “We expect the gross margin to be between 51% and 52%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “We are actually narrowing the window and also increasing the window of our expectation to EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion for this year.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “For the gross margin, we maintain our expectation of 51% to 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Compared with Q4 2025 guidance language, management moved from “EUR 34 billion and EUR 39 billion” for 2026 to “EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion,” while keeping the 2026 gross margin range at “51% to 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • The provided analystsEstimates data was not used because it did not include a valid fiscalQuarter format (Q1–Q4) as required.

Financial results

  • “For the quarter, total net sales came in at EUR 8.8 billion.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “Included in the EUR 8.8 billion was EUR 2.5 billion for Installed Base revenue.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “If you look at the gross margin for Q1, 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “Net income for the quarter, EUR 2.8 billion.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Management tied the quarter’s margin to mix within Installed Base: “there were components in there that actually come in at quite some strong gross margins. So as a result of that, a pretty high gross margin at 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

Q&A

  • No analyst Q&A session was included in this transcript; the discussion was a company-led video format with prompts from Investor Relations.

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts sentiment could not be assessed because no analysts participated in the transcript.

  • Management tone was positive and capacity-focused in both prepared remarks and the IR-led exchanges, with assertive demand language such as “sold out for 2026” and “supply will not meet the demand for the foreseeable future.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

  • Versus the prior quarter’s tone that “the market outlook has notably improved in the last few months,” the current call reinforced that improvement with more specific constraint language around 2026 and beyond. (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Guidance shifted upward and narrowed: Q4 2025 included “EUR 34 billion and EUR 39 billion” for 2026 revenue, while Q1 2026 updated to “EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion,” with the 2026 gross margin expectation unchanged at “51% to 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Non-EUV expectations changed from “kind of flattish” (Q4 2025) to “an increase of demand” and “increased revenue on the non-EUV business” (Q1 2026), with Dassen citing immersion demand having “reversed itself.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Installed Base commentary remained oriented around upgrades and fast capacity adds; Q1 2026 emphasized this as “a very fast way for our customers to increase their capacity.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Technology roadmap updates became more specific on throughput and long-term extendability, including: “in 2031, we'll be able to run this tool at 330 wafers per hour,” and near-term NXE:3800E throughput moving “from 220 to 230 wafers per hour.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

Risks and concerns

  • Export controls were flagged as an uncertainty within full-year planning: “within the guidance that we provided, the EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion, we believe we can accommodate potential outcomes of the export control discussions that are currently ongoing.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Execution risk around demand reversals and ramp timing was implied in immersion: “we actually had a bit of a slow start… That has now reversed itself,” paired with the mitigation message that ASML still expects “pretty close to the immersion sales that we had last year in terms of unit numbers.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

Final takeaway

Management presented Q1 2026 as within its sales guidance while attributing margin strength to Installed Base mix, then raised and narrowed full-year 2026 net sales expectations to €36 billion–€40 billion while maintaining a 51%–53% gross margin range. Leadership framed demand as being driven by AI infrastructure and described customers as “sold out for 2026,” with capacity expansion plans supported by ASML targets of at least 60 Low NA EUV systems in 2026 and at least 80 in 2027 if demand supports it, alongside roadmap throughput gains and a stated ability to absorb potential export-control outcomes within the updated 2026 range.
